question.title: How do you change vs studio directory?
question.body:
I was trying to run my maven java project in the VSCode PowerShell terminal, with this command mvn package, but I get this error:

& : The term 'c:\Users\milop\OneDrive\Documents\Personal Github Repositories\StockAnalysis-1\libs\mvn.cmd' is not recognized as a name of a cmdlet, function, 
script file, or executable program. Check the spelling of the name, or if a path was included, verify that the path is correct and try again.

I noticed that the directory for mvn.cmd was completely wrong being: c:\Users\milop\OneDrive\Documents\Personal Github Repositories\StockAnalysis-1\libs\mvn.cmd and it should have been: C:\Program Files\apache-maven-3.9.5\bin\mvn.cmd

My question is how do I fix this error? I do not know if this has to do with this error but when I click dependencies in my maven project it says backround process terminated with code 1 error.

I tried looking back at my environmental variable on my computer but everything is right there. I also tried redownloading maven but no luck.

answers.body:
Edit the following setting:

"maven.executable.path": ""

Official documents, or search maven in the Settings panel for more related settings.
